# DataSourceDescriptions

The goal of DataSourceDescriptions is to …

## Installation

You can install the development version of DataSourceDescriptions from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("oxford-pharmacoepi/DataSourceDescriptions")
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to solve a common problem:

``` r

library(DataSourceDescriptions)
## basic example code
```

What is special about using `README.Rmd` instead of just `README.md`?
You can include R chunks like so:

``` r

summary(cars)
#>      speed           dist       
#>  Min.   : 4.0   Min.   :  2.00  
#>  1st Qu.:12.0   1st Qu.: 26.00  
#>  Median :15.0   Median : 36.00  
#>  Mean   :15.4   Mean   : 42.98  
#>  3rd Qu.:19.0   3rd Qu.: 56.00  
#>  Max.   :25.0   Max.   :120.00
```

You’ll still need to render `README.Rmd` regularly, to keep `README.md`
up-to-date. `devtools::build_readme()` is handy for this.

You can also embed plots, for example:

![](reference/figures/README-pressure-1.png)

In that case, don’t forget to commit and push the resulting figure
files, so they display on GitHub and CRAN.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`emptyDataSourceDescription()`](https://oxford-pharmacoepi.github.io/DataSourceDescriptions/reference/emptyDataSourceDescription.md)
  :

  Create an empty `data_source_description` object

- [`exportDataSourceDescription()`](https://oxford-pharmacoepi.github.io/DataSourceDescriptions/reference/exportDataSourceDescription.md)
  : Export data source descriptions

- [`importDataSourceDescription()`](https://oxford-pharmacoepi.github.io/DataSourceDescriptions/reference/importDataSourceDescription.md)
  : Import data source descriptions

- [`newDataSourceDescription()`](https://oxford-pharmacoepi.github.io/DataSourceDescriptions/reference/newDataSourceDescription.md)
  :

  `data_source_description` object constructor

- [`print(`*`<data_source_description>`*`)`](https://oxford-pharmacoepi.github.io/DataSourceDescriptions/reference/print.data_source_description.md)
  : Print a data source description
